source_note: USDA report dates per USDA WAOB/NASS and CME 2026 report calendar. All USDA times are Eastern. USDA may shift dates around federal holidays; re-verify annually against issuing-agency calendars. Crop-insurance milestones per USDA RMA.
usage_note: The calendar engine looks ahead `lead_days` from today and queues a card of `card_type`. `impact` drives headline ranking in the Haiku triage step (very_high > high > medium). `watch_template` and `history_note` are drafting inputs for the Sonnet synthesis step, not final copy. Every rendered card must carry the standard education footer (see BEANBRIEF_MARKETING_CONTEXT.md §2.3).

events.title: January WASDE + Winter Wheat Seedings + Dec 1 Grain Stocks
events.watch_template: The January WASDE lands alongside the final annual crop production numbers and December 1 quarterly stocks. It often resets the marketing-year balance sheet and can be volatile.
events.history_note: The January package (final production + stocks + WASDE) is one of the more market-moving report days of the winter.

events.title: Grain Stocks (December 1 inventory)
events.watch_template: Quarterly Grain Stocks measures bushels on farms and in commercial storage as of December 1. It's a read on the pace of usage (implied demand) so far this marketing year.
events.history_note: Stocks reports can surprise via implied-demand math even when acreage and yield are settled.

events.title: February WASDE
events.watch_template: The February WASDE updates supply and demand but predates spring planting intentions and new-crop estimates.
events.history_note: February WASDE reports tend to be lower-drama than the spring and summer editions.

events.title: Crop-insurance spring price discovery window
events.watch_template: Through February, the average of daily settlements for December corn and November soybean futures sets the crop-insurance spring (projected) price. This establishes the revenue-guarantee floor for the year.
events.history_note: February has been the calendar-year price high for new-crop November soybeans (or December corn) only once since 1994 — a spike during discovery has rarely been the year's best.

events.title: Crop-insurance spring (projected) price set
events.watch_template: The 2026 spring projected prices are set: soybeans $11.09, corn $4.62, spring wheat $6.19. These, times your APH yield and coverage level, define your revenue guarantee — a key input for sizing pre-harvest marketing off insured bushels.
events.history_note: On RP with the harvest-price option, the guarantee later settles on the higher of this spring price or the October harvest price.

events.title: March WASDE
events.watch_template: The March WASDE is the last monthly balance sheet before planting intentions and new-crop projections arrive at month-end.

events.title: Crop-insurance sales closing date (most Corn Belt)
events.watch_template: March 15 is the sales closing date for corn and soybean policies across most of the Corn Belt — the deadline to buy, change, or cancel coverage for the 2026 crop.

events.title: Prospective Plantings
events.watch_template: Prospective Plantings reports farmers' intended corn, soybean, and wheat acres from the March survey. It sets the tone for the entire new-crop marketing year and is often one of the most volatile report days of spring.
events.history_note: Because it's the first hard acreage signal of the year, surprises here can move new-crop futures sharply.

events.title: Grain Stocks (March 1 inventory)
events.watch_template: March 1 quarterly stocks release the same day as Prospective Plantings, compounding the day's volatility. It updates the marketing-year usage pace.
events.history_note: The March 31 double-header (Plantings + Stocks) is a classic high-volatility date.

events.title: April WASDE
events.watch_template: The April WASDE refines old-crop balance sheets as spring planting begins.

events.title: May WASDE (first new-crop supply & demand)
events.watch_template: The May WASDE carries the first full supply-and-demand estimates for the new 2026/27 marketing year, folding in the March planting intentions. It frames the new-crop balance sheet the market trades all summer.
events.history_note: The first new-crop WASDE is a marquee report; it also lands right at the front edge of the historical mid-May-to-mid-June pricing window.

events.title: June WASDE
events.watch_template: The June WASDE updates new-crop projections just ahead of the pivotal end-of-June acreage and stocks reports.

events.title: Acreage (June survey of planted acres)
events.watch_template: Acreage reports actual planted corn and soybean acres from the June 1 survey — frequently the single largest one-day price mover of the entire crop year. Watch for fund 'evening up' in the days beforehand.
events.history_note: Analysts widely regard the June 30 Acreage report as the biggest scheduled market event of the year; pre-report positioning can itself move prices.

events.title: Grain Stocks (June 1 inventory)
events.watch_template: June 1 quarterly stocks release alongside Acreage, making June 30 a compounded high-volatility day. It's a late-marketing-year usage check.
events.history_note: The June 30 Acreage + Stocks double-header is typically the most volatile single day on the grain calendar.

events.title: July WASDE
events.watch_template: The July WASDE incorporates the fresh end-of-June acreage figures into the new-crop balance sheet.
events.history_note: Lands just after the historical early-July checkpoint for wrapping up pre-harvest marketing.

events.title: August WASDE + first survey-based yields
events.watch_template: The August WASDE / Crop Production delivers the first field-survey-based corn and soybean yield estimates, replacing model-based forecasts. It can substantially reset the production outlook.
events.history_note: The first survey yield is a major report; August is also when the market starts asking whether China returns for U.S. new crop.

events.title: Crop-insurance premium billing date
events.watch_template: August 15 is the standard premium billing date for corn, soybean, and spring wheat policies.

events.title: September WASDE
events.watch_template: The September WASDE updates yield as harvest begins and demand signals firm up.

events.title: Grain Stocks (September 1 inventory) — final old-crop carryout
events.watch_template: The September 1 stocks report is the most critical of the four quarterly stocks reports: it sets the baseline old-crop carryout that anchors new-crop pricing direction.
events.history_note: September stocks routinely surprise and can swing prices as the market locks in the beginning-of-year balance.

events.title: October WASDE
events.watch_template: The October WASDE updates harvest-time yield and demand as combines run.

events.title: Crop-insurance harvest price discovery window
events.watch_template: Through October, the average of December corn and November soybean settlements sets the crop-insurance harvest price. On RP with the harvest-price option, the guarantee uses the higher of this or the February spring price.
events.history_note: When the October harvest price exceeds February's spring price, RP-HPO guarantees rise — the mechanic behind why early pre-harvest sales on insured acres carry replacement protection.

events.title: November WASDE (soybean production finalized)
events.watch_template: The November WASDE typically finalizes current-year soybean production and updates the balance sheet as harvest wraps.
events.history_note: Final soybean production numbers can move the market if they diverge from the running estimate.

events.title: Crop-insurance harvest price set
events.watch_template: The 2026 harvest price is set from October's average. Compare it to the February spring price to understand your final RP guarantee and any harvest-price-option adjustment.

events.title: December WASDE
events.watch_template: The December WASDE updates old-crop demand and sets up the January final-production package.

recurring_events.title: Crop Progress & Condition
recurring_events.watch_template: Weekly condition ratings (excellent/good/fair/poor/very poor) and planting or harvest progress by state. Background context on crop health; elevate to a card only when a condition trigger references it (e.g., a sharp rating change during the summer weather window).
recurring_events.note: Do not push as a standalone card by default (triage rule §7.5). Use as supporting context.

recurring_events.title: Weekly Export Sales (FAS)
recurring_events.watch_template: Weekly net export sales of corn, soybeans, and wheat to foreign buyers. Large China purchases routinely move futures on release. Primary data source for the China seasonal-demand clock.
recurring_events.note: Background context by default; elevate when the china_demand_clock trigger is active (esp. the August new-crop return watch).
